session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8513 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute the PR feedback classifier for PR #8513.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback using the erk exec command with --pr 8513:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch PR feedback and comments for PR 8513

session.tool_result:
{ "success": true, "pr_number": 8513, "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8513", "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from Feb 28 learn sessions (batch 2)", "review_threads": [ { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xcsiO", "path": "docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md", "line": 21, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — line numbers in source pointer.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py`

The HTML comment mixes a line range (`:715-725`) with a symbol name (`RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data`). Per `docs/learned/documentation/source-pointers.md`, prefer name-based identifiers over line numbers — names survive refactoring while line numbers go stale silently.

Suggested fix: Remove the line range, keep the symbol name:
```
<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py, RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data -->
```", "created_at": "2026-03-02T02:14:04Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xcslQ", "path": "docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md", "line": 23, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — line numbers in prose reference.

Source: `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/plan_data_provider/real.py`

Risk: `real.py:715-725` will go stale silently when any edit shifts those lines. The symbol name alone is sufficient and more stable.

Suggested fix: Remove the line range from prose:
> See `RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data()` in `real.py` for the dual-source detection logic.", "created_at": "2026-03-02T02:14:11Z" }

[View workflow run](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22532232001)", "body": "<!-- audit-pr-docs -->

## ❌ Audit PR Docs

**Last updated:** 2026-03-01 18:14:15 PT

Found 2 violations across 1 file. Inline comments posted for each.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ Verbatim code blocks — None found
✅ Inaccurate claims — None found (all source pointers verified)
❌ Drift risk (line numbers in source pointers) — Found in docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md:21,23

### Violations Summary
- `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md:21`: Source pointer HTML comment mixes line range `:715-725` with symbol name — prefer name-based identifier only
- `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md:23`: Prose reference `real.py:715-725` includes line numbers that will go stale

### Files Reviewed
- `docs/learned/architecture/index.md`: 0 violations (index entry update only)
- `docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md`: 0 violations (new section clean — bash block is CLI invocation exception)
- `docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md`: 0 violations (previous verbatim blocks replaced with source pointers)
- `docs/learned/planning/index.md`: 0 violations (index entry addition only)
- `docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md`: 0 violations (new doc uses source pointers correctly)
- `docs/learned/tui/index.md`: 0 violations (index entry addition only)
- `docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md`: 0 violations (new doc — design decisions are HIGH VALUE, source pointers correct)
- `docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md`: 2 violations (line numbers in source pointer and prose)

---

### Activity Log
- **2026-03-01 18:14:15 PT**: Audited 8 docs: 7 KEEP, 1 with drift risk violations (2 line-number references in stacked-pr-indicator.md). Skipped 0 duplicate comments (all 16 prior threads were outdated/resolved).

</details>", "body": "<!-- erk:pr-address-run -->
## PR Review Comments Addressed

This PR was updated by an automated process triggered by:
```
erk pr address-remote 8513
```

### What is this?

[Erk](https://github.com/schrockn/erk) is a CLI tool for plan-oriented agentic
engineering. The `pr-address` workflow uses Claude to read PR review comments
and address them automatically.

### Changes Made

The PR review comments corrected inaccurate source code references by replacing specific line numbers with function names. Two documentation files were updated:

- **land-learn-integration.md**: Replaced a verbatim code block with a reference to the `_create_learn_pr_with_sessions()` function, keeping the semantic explanation of fire-and-forget error handling.
- **learn-session-embedding.md**: Changed source pointers from specific line ranges to the `_save_as_planned_pr()` function name, and simplified the explanation to focus on the logic rather than implementation details.

These changes make the documentation more maintainable by referencing stable function names instead of line numbers that may shift during refactoring.

### Details

- **Model:** claude-opus-4-6
- **Workflow run:** [View details](https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22558426119)
- **Commits added:** 2

---
*Automated by erk pr-address workflow*",
session.assistant: Now let me get the file restructuring context:

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C master...HEAD
Get file restructuring context for the PR

session.tool_result: docs/learned/architecture/index.md | 2 +- docs/learned/architecture/inference-hoisting.md | 36 +++++++++++++ docs/learned/cli/land-learn-integration.md | 66 ++++++++++++++---------- docs/learned/planning/index.md | 1 + docs/learned/planning/learn-session-embedding.md | 64 +++++++++++++++++++++++ docs/learned/tui/index.md | 1 + docs/learned/tui/one-shot-prompt-modal.md | 63 ++++++++++++++++++++++ docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md | 22 +++++--- 8 files changed, 219 insertions(+), 36 deletions(-)
session.assistant:
Perfect. Now I'll classify the feedback according to the Comment Classification Model. Let me analyze:
**Review Threads Analysis:**
1. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xcsiO** (line 21, stacked-pr-indicator.md): - Author: github-actions (bot) - Issue: Source pointer HTML comment mixes line range `:715-725` with symbol name - Classification: Actionable (bot suggestion for documentation improvement) - Pre-existing: TRUE - This is a bot comment on modified documentation code. The file is being modified (shown in git diff), and the issue is about generic documentation quality (line numbers in source pointers), not something caused by the restructuring itself. However, per the skill definition, pre-existing requires: bot author (YES), file restructuring (YES), and flagged pattern generic to original location (YES - the same documentation standard would apply to the original file if it existed). - Complexity: local (single line) - Action: Remove line range from HTML comment
2. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xcslQ** (line 23, stacked-pr-indicator.md): - Author: github-actions (bot) - Issue: Prose reference includes line numbers that will go stale - Classification: Actionable (bot suggestion for documentation improvement) - Pre-existing: TRUE - Same reasoning as above - Complexity: local (single line) - Action: Remove line range from prose
**Discussion Comments Analysis:**
All discussion comments are informational: - 3978698375: Metadata about plan queued - 3978699057: Workflow started notification - 3978723660: Audit PR Docs summary (CI status) - 3980819868: PR review comments addressed (automation notification) - 3980951392: Remote rebase completed (automation notification) - 3981615975: Violations addressed confirmation (automation notification)

Suggested fix: Remove the line range, keep the symbol name:" }, { "thread_id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5xcslQ", "type": "review", "path": "docs/learned/tui/stacked-pr-indicator.md", "line": 23, "is_outdated": false, "classification": "actionable", "pre_existing": true, "action_summary": "Remove line range from prose reference, use symbol name only", "complexity": "local", "original_comment": "**Audit PR Docs**: Drift risk — line numbers in prose reference.
